meta.subtitle: Roadmap
meta.currentState: The roadmap now combines operating instructions, goals, command rules, the decision ledger, a complete agent-planning standard, and a stage-gated project-management workflow. Existing implementation remains local and unchanged except for this planning UI.
meta.nextFocus: Complete the 12-field blueprint for each of the ten AI agents, starting with Aims Captain (Manager), then freeze cross-agent handoffs and the shared project lifecycle before further specialist coding.

stats.label: Overall
stats.detail: The dashboard and Manager Agent local MVP exist, and the cloud rollout has started with GitHub as source of truth plus Codespaces and Vercel repository configuration. Hosted data, voice, and background execution remain pending.

stats.label: Agents
stats.detail: Ten agent roles are mapped as requirements, not implemented services.

stats.label: Ready
stats.detail: The roadmap dashboard, Manager Agent local MVP, and Manager routing/approval eval suite are ready for review.

stats.label: Decision Items
stats.detail: These decisions should be frozen before core development begins. API billing/quota is now tracked because live OpenAI calls reached the API but were rejected by current quota.

agents.name: Manager
agents.purpose: Central dispatcher for requests, routing, approvals, schedules, and operational visibility.
todos: Run live OpenAI smoke test after billing/quota is unblocked
Add run-log dashboard filters for agent, status, and approval state
Promote file-store schema to PostgreSQL when production begins
risks: Live OpenAI validation is blocked until API billing/quota is fixed
Do not connect publishing or client-write tools until approvals are tested in live mode
agents.statusAction: Local Manager MVP is ready. Next blocker: enable API billing/quota and run the live OpenAI smoke test.

agents.name: Develop
agents.purpose: Builds web/app work, writes tests, opens pull requests, and prepares deployments.
agents.compact: Code, review, test, PR, deploy handoff.
todos: Connect the private repository to Vercel and verify production deployment
Create the Supabase project and versioned migrations
risks: Production changes without review
Client repo credentials must stay isolated
agents.statusAction: The authenticated Manager and Realtime API boundaries are implemented; the active work is production voice verification.

agents.name: Design
agents.purpose: Produces UI directions, brand systems, campaign visuals, and handoff-ready creative briefs.
risks: Off-brand outputs
Unlicensed assets entering client work
agents.statusAction: Freeze the client brand kit schema after the Manager Agent requirements are stable.

agents.name: Video
agents.purpose: Plans short-form and campaign video scripts, storyboards, captions, and edit briefs.
risks: Wrong claims in ad scripts
Missing permissions for client media
agents.statusAction: Keep planned until creative asset and approval structures are defined.

agents.name: Google
agents.purpose: Handles SEO, Google Ads planning, keyword strategy, landing-page checks, and performance reports.
risks: Ad spend changes need strict permission
Analytics data can be noisy or delayed
agents.statusAction: Start with read-only reporting before any ad budget action is allowed.

agents.name: Social
agents.purpose: Creates platform-specific calendars, posts, hooks, campaigns, and approval-ready drafts.
todos: Detect schedule collisions per platform and timezone
risks: Publishing without approval
Tone mismatch across clients
agents.statusAction: Content schema, approval-gated lifecycle, and caption rubric are tested; add brand context and calendar validation next.

agents.name: Sales
agents.purpose: Researches leads, drafts outreach, prepares proposals, and keeps pipeline records current.
risks: Outbound messages need approval
Lead data must be sourced responsibly
agents.statusAction: Choose the first CRM and map required pipeline fields.

agents.name: Support
agents.purpose: Handles client follow-ups, weekly summaries, support requests, and account health signals.
risks: Client-facing messages need approval
Support issues can hide delivery risks
agents.statusAction: Define support lifecycle after Manager task states are connected.

agents.name: QA
agents.purpose: Reviews output quality, factual accuracy, brand fit, permissions, and publish readiness.
risks: Weak governance makes every other agent unsafe
Review rules can become too vague
agents.statusAction: Create the first cross-agent QA checklist before any publishing tools are connected.

agents.name: Accounts
agents.purpose: Tracks invoices, retainers, subscriptions, payment follow-ups, and project profitability notes.
risks: Financial messages require approval
Sensitive payment data must stay protected
agents.statusAction: Define accounting workflow after core CRM and client records are selected.

phases.title: Requirement Freeze
phases.summary: Use one operating-plan area for instructions, goals, decisions, agent blueprints, project workflow, requirements, tasks, and evidence. The planning schema and approval rules are defined; detailed per-agent blueprints remain to be completed.
phases.todo: Complete and review all ten agent blueprints, then freeze shared handoffs, data contracts, and definition-of-done rules.
phases.statusAction: Next: fully specify Aims Captain using the 12-field blueprint, then repeat in dependency order for every specialist agent.

phases.title: Independent Core
phases.summary: Build owned backend, database, scheduler, queue, and file/memory foundations. Manager Agent service endpoints, file-backed task/run persistence, and dashboard run mirroring are implemented.
phases.todo: Promote the local file schema to PostgreSQL when production storage begins.
phases.statusAction: Production persistence direction is PostgreSQL + pgvector after the local file-store schema is proven.

phases.title: Agent MVP
phases.summary: Ship Manager, Social Market, and Sales agents with tool calls and approval gates. Manager is ready, and Social now has a tested content schema, approval-gated lifecycle, and caption rubric.
phases.todo: Add Social client brand-profile validation and calendar collision checks; resume live Realtime verification when quota resets.
phases.statusAction: Social is the active API-free specialist build while live OpenAI checks are quota-blocked.

phases.title: Delivery Agents
phases.summary: Add Development, Design, Video, and Google Market workflows after the control layer works.
phases.todo: Define safe tool permissions per specialist.
phases.statusAction: Start after MVP agents are reliable.

phases.title: Agency OS
phases.summary: Unify dashboards, reports, costs, schedules, evals, and role-based access.
phases.statusAction: Start after core workflows are proven.

phases.title: Cloud Workspace
phases.summary: The existing private GitHub repository is the source of truth, Codespaces configuration is present, CI validates cloud changes, and Vercel production deployment is working at https://ai-ms.vercel.app/.
phases.todo: Verify Codespaces from GitHub and complete Supabase integration before moving application state online.
phases.statusAction: Vercel deployment is live; Supabase is the active cloud setup step.

phases.title: Hosted Roadmap Data
phases.summary: Supabase is connected to GitHub, secured schema migrations apply successfully, and the production frontend loads the hosted aims-roadmap document with a bundled fallback.
phases.todo: Keep future schema and seed changes versioned through GitHub migrations.
phases.statusAction: Hosted roadmap data is complete for the current cloud foundation.

phases.title: Online Manager API
phases.summary: The private login gate, owner session, protected Vercel Roadmap and Manager endpoints, server configuration, admin allowlist, version-checked roadmap writes, and authenticated local roadmap loading are implemented. Production correctly rejects unauthenticated requests with HTTP 401.
phases.todo: Keep the protected endpoints and owner allowlist covered by deployment smoke checks.
phases.statusAction: Online Manager API is complete for the current roadmap foundation.

phases.title: Hold-to-Talk Voice
phases.summary: Authenticated press-and-hold capture now opens a short-lived OpenAI Realtime WebRTC session, streams transcription into the Manager command bar, and requires explicit confirmation for consequential commands.
phases.todo: Run an authenticated production microphone smoke test and document any browser-specific permission or connection issues.
phases.statusAction: Code complete; active next focus is production microphone and Realtime session verification.

phases.title: Background Work
phases.summary: Run bounded cloud tasks, scheduled status reports, CI checks, and approved maintenance while the owner's laptop is offline.
phases.todo: Define safe scheduled jobs and connect GitHub Actions or Codex cloud tasks with audit logs and approval gates.
phases.statusAction: Starts after online state, authentication, and command approvals are proven.

stack.name: React + Tailwind
stack.next: Use this dashboard as the requirements surface.

stack.name: OpenAI Responses API
stack.next: Manager Agent live mode is coded, but live output remains blocked until API quota or billing is enabled.

stack.name: PostgreSQL + pgvector
stack.next: Selected production persistence direction after the local file-backed task/run schema is validated.

stack.name: Redis or open-source queue
stack.next: Add for scheduled and background agent jobs.

stack.name: Docker
stack.next: Use for local database and service orchestration.

stack.name: GitHub + Codex

stack.name: Vercel
stack.next: Production deployment is live at https://ai-ms.vercel.app/ and follows the connected GitHub repository.

stack.name: Supabase

stack.name: OpenAI Realtime API
stack.next: Verify the authenticated WebRTC session, live transcription, and confirmation gate in production with microphone permission.

workRound.title: AiMS Operating Plan & Instructions
workRound.purpose: Keep goals, agreed operating rules, agent blueprints, project workflow, and the decision ledger in one living planning area. Every discussion that changes scope, priority, permissions, architecture, or completion criteria must be reflected here before dependent implementation continues.
goals: Build AiMS as a safe agency operating system in which a Manager Agent coordinates specialist agents, shared context, approvals, schedules, and auditable work.
Plan every agent completely before granting tools or production access: purpose, inputs, outputs, authority, workflow, quality gates, metrics, risks, and rollout must be explicit.
Use one roadmap as the source of truth for decisions, priorities, progress, dependencies, blockers, and the next focused work package.
Keep consequential actions human-approved and privileged credentials server-side; begin integrations read-only wherever practical.
Deliver in small verified increments, keep changes local by default, and deploy only when the owner explicitly says `deploy aim`.

commands.action: Complete only the highest-priority established roadmap item, verify it, update this plan, and leave changes local.
Define the next work package, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and checks without implementing it.
Run relevant health checks and report findings without making fixes.
Review local changes for defects, security, regressions, and missing tests before further work.
Report current progress, local changes, blockers, decisions needed, and the recommended next focus.
After final review and passing checks, commit the intended local changes and push them to GitHub. This is the only deployment trigger.

triggerModes: Discussion or decision: update the instructions, decision ledger, goals, agent plan, dependencies, or acceptance criteria affected by the conversation.
Direct work request: complete the smallest coherent requested package, verify it, and synchronize roadmap state without committing or pushing.
Next-focus request: use nextFocus, priority, dependencies, and decided scope to complete one highest-value package, then stop.
Deployment request: act only when the owner says the exact command `deploy aim`; failed checks stop deployment.
steps: Read the operating plan, decision ledger, relevant agent blueprint, roadmap state, and repository state.
Classify new discussion points as a goal, decided rule, suggestion, pending decision, requirement, dependency, risk, or task and update the roadmap accordingly.
Select one focus with a clear owner, scope, deliverable, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and verification plan.
Implement only when requested; preserve unrelated work and stop at approval boundaries.
Verify the result with targeted tests, build checks, security review, and browser validation as applicable.
Update agent and phase progress, decisions, blockers, evidence, and exactly one recommended next focus.
Report locally completed work. Do not commit or push unless the owner separately says `deploy aim`.
agentPlanningFields: Identity, business purpose, owner, and measurable outcome
Users, trigger events, supported requests, and exclusions
Required inputs, client context, memory, and data freshness
Outputs, schemas, templates, storage location, and retention
Tools and integrations with read, draft, write, publish, and admin permissions
Step-by-step happy path plus alternate, retry, timeout, and failure paths
Human approval gates, approvers, expiry, cancellation, and escalation
Handoffs to Manager, QA, and other specialist agents
Quality rubric, factual checks, brand checks, safety policy, and eval cases
KPIs, service levels, cost limits, observability, and audit-log fields
Security, privacy, credentials, tenant isolation, and data-deletion rules
MVP boundary, dependencies, rollout stages, test fixtures, and definition of done

projectWorkflow.outcome: Convert discussion into a problem statement, desired outcome, stakeholders, constraints, and evidence.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: Owner and success condition are clear; unknowns are recorded instead of assumed.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Record architecture, scope, permission, priority, and product decisions in the decision ledger.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: Blocking choices are decided or explicitly marked pending with an owner and dependency.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Complete the agent blueprint, workflow states, data contracts, approval gates, risks, and acceptance criteria.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: The work can be implemented and tested without inventing material behavior.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Break delivery into dependency-ordered focus packages with one owner and one verifiable result each.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: The next focus is unambiguous, bounded, unblocked, and has a verification plan.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Implement the smallest useful vertical slice while preserving security and approval boundaries.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: Code and documentation satisfy the focus acceptance criteria locally.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Run automated checks, scenario/eval cases, security review, and user-visible validation where applicable.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: Evidence is recorded; failures are fixed or the item remains blocked/in process.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Confirm scope, quality, progress, risks, decisions, and the next focus with the owner.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: Owner accepts the result or feedback is converted into a new bounded focus.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Commit and push only under `deploy aim`, then verify the resulting deployment and record evidence.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: Checks pass, intended files only are committed, deployment is healthy, and rollback is understood.

projectWorkflow.outcome: Monitor quality, cost, failures, approvals, security, and business KPIs; feed learning back into planning.
projectWorkflow.exitCriteria: Recurring ownership, alerts, review cadence, and improvement backlog are active.

workRound.lastCompleted: Converted the long roadmap into a compact four-page workspace with global Manager commands, an actionable overview, focused AI Team and Delivery pages, and a unified Operating Plan with workflows and decisions.

approvalMatrix.approver: Business owner or assigned account lead
Business owner or client-approved content lead

approvalMatrix.action: Create drafts, reports, briefs, or internal tasks

decisions.label: Workspace information architecture
decisions.detail: Decided. Use four compact workspace pages: Overview for current focus and health, AI Team for agent blueprints and authority, Delivery for phases and technology, and Operating Plan for instructions, workflows, commands, and decisions. Keep the Manager command bar globally available.

decisions.label: CRM choice
decisions.detail: Pending. Choose the first CRM target before Sales Agent implementation. Recommended low-dependency path: start with a simple internal pipeline table, then connect HubSpot, Zoho, or Pipedrive only if needed.

decisions.label: MVP agent order
decisions.detail: Decided. Build order starts with Agency Manager, then Social Market and Sales. This gives routing, approvals, content planning, and lead workflow before deeper integrations.

decisions.label: Approval roles
decisions.detail: Decided for MVP. Client email, publishing, ad budget changes, deployment, and production data edits require human approval. Drafts, reports, briefs, and internal task creation may proceed with logs.

decisions.label: Client file schema
decisions.detail: Pending. Define the required client knowledge folders: brand, offers, services, assets, reports, meetings, approvals, and credentials references.

decisions.label: Backend language
decisions.detail: Decided. Use Node first because this machine already has Node and the roadmap dashboard is Vite/React. The Manager Agent can call the OpenAI Responses API directly now; Python/FastAPI can be added later only if a specialist workflow needs it.

decisions.label: Weekly reports
decisions.detail: Pending. Decide the first recurring report format: client summary, active tasks, wins, blockers, next-week plan, and metrics snapshot.

decisions.label: API billing quota
decisions.detail: Pending. Live OpenAI smoke test reached the API but returned a current quota/billing error. Enable API billing or adjust usage limits before live agent runs can complete.

decisions.label: Production persistence
decisions.detail: Decided. Use PostgreSQL + pgvector as the production persistence backend after the local file-backed task and run-log schema is validated. Keep the JSON file store as the local prototype and dashboard mirror.
